question: [File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user perform a man-in-the-middle attack on the HTTP connection to the mirror node REST API, given that fetchMirrorNodeNetwork() uses http.Client with no TLS configuration (mirror_node_client.go line 46) and the mirrorRest URL may use plain HTTP, to inject a crafted nodesEnvelope response that maps all consensus node addresses to attacker-controlled endpoints, redirecting all staking reward pool transfers?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_TO_ACCOUNT_ID to '0.0.800' (the Hedera staking reward account on mainnet), given that loadConfig() performs no validation that the destination is not a protected system account, causing the pinger to send HBAR to the wrong staking account and bypass the intended 0.0.98 reward distribution mechanism, misdirecting unpaid staking rewards?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that submitWithRetry() calls hiero.AccountIDFromString(cfg.toAccountID) on every tick (transfer.go line 15) and returns an error without stopping the ticker if parsing fails, given that a malformed toAccountID would cause every tick to fail silently (main.go line 67-68), to permanently disable staking reward pool funding by injecting an invalid account ID string?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the retry loop in buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() (mirror_node_client.go lines 52-69) by serving a mirror node that always returns HTTP 429 or 5xx, given that the retry logic uses exponential backoff but will eventually exhaust all attempts, causing newClient() to fail and the pinger to exit without ever submitting staking reward pool transfers, creating a permanent denial of staking rewards?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_MIRROR_NODE_CLIENT_TIMEOUT to '0s', given that loadConfig() resets mirrorNodeClientTimeout to 10s only when <= 0 (config.go line 129-131), but toDuration('0s') returns 0 which triggers the reset, to manipulate the HTTP client timeout and cause all mirror node requests to time out immediately, preventing the pinger from building a valid node topology and blocking staking reward pool transfers?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a domain_name value containing special characters (e.g., newlines, null bytes) into the service_endpoints of the mirror node response, given that fetchMirrorNodeNetwork() only calls strings.TrimSpace() on the domain name (mirror_node_client.go line 114) before passing it to net.JoinHostPort(), causing malformed gRPC endpoint addresses that the SDK resolves to attacker-controlled hosts?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the pinger writes /tmp/alive every 15 seconds (main.go line 36) with world-readable permissions (0644), to monitor the file's modification time from a co-located container and infer the exact timing of the pinger's heartbeat goroutine, then time an attack to coincide with a window between heartbeats when the pinger is busy processing a transfer retry, delaying staking reward pool funding?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_AMOUNT_TINYBAR to the minimum int64 value (-9223372036854775808), given that loadConfig() only rejects zero (config.go line 111-113), causing AddHbarTransfer(operatorID, hiero.HbarFromTinybar(9223372036854775808)) to overflow and produce an unexpected transfer amount that corrupts the staking reward pool accounting?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_NETWORK to 'mainnet' while the pinger is intended for testnet, given that loadConfig() accepts any of testnet/previewnet/mainnet/other without environment-specific validation (config.go line 36), causing the pinger to submit real HBAR transfers on mainnet to account 0.0.98 using the default genesis key, potentially draining real mainnet staking rewards?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user serve a mirror node response with duplicate node_account_id entries mapping the same account to different attacker-controlled endpoints, given that fetchMirrorNodeNetwork() uses a map[string]hiero.AccountID (mirror_node_client.go line 100) where later entries overwrite earlier ones, causing the SDK to route staking reward pool transfers to the last-seen (attacker-controlled) endpoint for each node account?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the envOr() function (config.go lines 140-145) which uses strings.TrimSpace() but does not sanitize shell metacharacters or injection sequences in environment variable values, to inject a crafted operatorKey or toAccountID that causes the hiero SDK to parse an unexpected key format and sign transactions with a different key than intended, redirecting staking reward pool transfers?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a maximum transfer amount cap in submitWithRetry(), given that cfg.amountTinybar is set from HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_AMOUNT_TINYBAR with no upper bound validation in loadConfig(), to set an astronomically large transfer amount that immediately drains the operator account in a single tick, preventing any further staking reward pool funding?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the signal handling in main() (main.go line 24) where only os.Interrupt and syscall.SIGTERM are handled, by sending SIGKILL to the pinger process from a co-located container with appropriate permissions, causing the pinger to terminate without graceful shutdown and leaving in-flight staking reward pool transfers in an unknown state?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the URL suffix detection logic in buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() (mirror_node_client.go lines 40-44) where the URL is '/api/v1/network/nodes' only if the base does not already end with '/api/v1', by crafting a mirrorRest value ending with '/api/v1' that points to an attacker-controlled server, causing the pinger to fetch node topology from 'http://attacker.com/api/v1/network/nodes' and receive malicious node mappings?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_OPERATOR_ID to a non-existent or deleted account ID, given that loadConfig() only validates non-empty (config.go line 102-104) and newClient() only validates parseable format (sdk_client.go line 35-38), causing all CryptoTransfer transactions to fail with INVALID_ACCOUNT_ID and permanently blocking staking reward pool funding?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user serve a mirror node response with an ip_address_v4 field containing an internal/private IP address (e.g., 169.254.169.254 for cloud metadata), given that fetchMirrorNodeNetwork() performs no SSRF protection on the IP addresses extracted from service_endpoints (mirror_node_client.go lines 113-124), causing the pinger's SDK to make gRPC connections to internal infrastructure endpoints?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the pinger's ticker interval (cfg.interval) and transfer amount (cfg.amountTinybar) are both logged at startup (main.go line 51-52) and readable from container logs, to calculate the exact rate of HBAR flowing into the staking reward pool and time a competing transaction to drain 0.0.98 just before the pinger's next scheduled transfer?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_MIRROR_NODE_CLIENT_MAX_RETRIES to a very large value, given that loadConfig() only resets negative values (config.go line 123-125) but does not cap positive values, causing buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() to retry indefinitely against an attacker-controlled mirror node that serves progressively different node topologies on each retry, eventually poisoning the network map with malicious endpoints?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that submitWithRetry() does not set a transaction memo or unique identifier on the CryptoTransfer (transfer.go lines 29-31), making it impossible to distinguish legitimate staking reward pool transfers from attacker-crafted transfers with the same parameters, allowing an attacker to replay or duplicate transfers that appear identical to the pinger's legitimate transactions?
